evals.prompt: The search skill failed to find "config*.json" when the file exists in a subdirectory. Run a full RCA analysis.
evals.expected_output: Must include: incident title with ID, timeline with timestamps, root cause section, classification (type/severity/scope), prevention with 2+ concrete actions, lessons learned, and next step

expectations.text: Contains INCIDENT header with ID number
Contains timeline with at least 3 timestamped entries
Root cause identifies glob (non-recursive) as the issue
Classification includes type=Error (not Violation or Negligence)
Prevention has at least 2 concrete actionable items

evals.prompt: Analyze: A developer knew temp files fail on Windows but used them anyway to save time during development. Run RCA.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as Violation, not Error. Must explain that the person knew the rule and deviated intentionally.

expectations.text: Explanation mentions intentional deviation despite known risk
Severity is proportional (not Critical — no data loss or security issue)
Prevention focuses on process controls, not training

evals.prompt: Quick analysis: the formatting skill crashed when given a null input. Keep it brief.
evals.expected_output: 3-question lite format: What happened, How fix, How prevent — each 1 line.

expectations.text: Contains only 3 sections (or clearly marked as lite)
What happened is 1 line describing the crash
How fix is 1 line with specific solution
How prevent is 1 line with preventive action

evals.prompt: We already saw this search incident twice in this repository. Use prior incident memory if relevant, run the RCA, and save the result in the right scope.
evals.expected_output: Must mention memory recall, use project scope, and include a Memory Actions section showing scope and save result.

expectations.text: Uses project scope for repository-specific incident memory
Mentions related incidents found or prior memory consulted
Saves result after RCA rather than treating memory as optional
Suggests a stable topic_key for the memory entry
